title: Past Continuous
usage: Действие продолжалось в определенный момент в прошлом

rules.example: I was reading when you called.

videoQuiz1.text: Are they shaking their hands right now?
videoQuiz1.explanation: Rachel is describing something that happened in the past.

videoQuiz1.text: The “saying goodbye and shaking hands” refers to…
videoQuiz1.explanation: Rachel is describing something that happened in the past.

videoQuiz1.text: “Saying goodbye” happened at once or it lasted for a certain period?
options: lasted for a certain time period
videoQuiz1.explanation:
They were saying goodbye and shaking hands for a couple of minutes at least. We use Past Continuous to talk about actions that were in progress in the past.
Example: I was driving home yesterday at 2 pm.

videoQuiz1.text: When the interviewer leaned towards Rachel…
options: they were still shaking their hands
videoQuiz1.explanation:
We also use Past Continuous to talk about actions that were interrupted by another in the past.
Example: I was washing my hands when the water suddenly stopped pouring.

grammarBank1.title: Past Continuous
grammarBank1.intro:
We use Past Continuous to:
  • talk about actions that were in progress in past;
  • to describe an event happened in the past;
  • to show that one action in progress was interrupted by another short action.
examples: 1) We were saying goodbye and we were shaking our hands.
2) It was summer. The sun was shining. The birds were singing.
3) He leaned towards me when we were shaking our hands.

videoQuiz1b.text:
2) Look at the sentences:
We were shaking hands.   He was going to open the door.
Which are the main verbs in the sentences?
videoQuiz1b.explanation: shaking and going are the main verbs. Was and were are auxiliary (helping) verbs.

videoQuiz1b.text:
What is the ending of the main verbs in the sentences?
(We were shaking hands.   He was going to open the door.)
videoQuiz1b.explanation:
In Past Continuous we make sentences by adding -ing to the verbs.
Rachel was talkING.

videoQuiz1b.text: What are the auxiliary verbs in Past Continuous?
videoQuiz1b.explanation:
The auxiliary verbs in Past Continuous are was and were.
Iwas sleeping
Youwere running
Hewas exercising
Shewas working
Itwas raining
Wewere dancing
Theywere eating

videoQuiz1b.text: Put the words in correct order:
videoQuiz1b.correct: Rachel was talking about her interview.

videoQuiz1b.text: Put the words in correct order:

blocks.intro:
The word order in positive Past Continuous sentences is SAV.
Subject + was/were + verb(ing).

videoQuiz2.text:
Focus on the sentence: “You sure you weren’t looking at my man Randy?”
Which word makes this sentence negative?
videoQuiz2.explanation:
We add not to the auxiliary verbs to make negative sentences.
Example: We were NOT looking at the boys.

videoQuiz2.text: Look at the word “weren’t”. What is the full form of this contraction?

options: after auxiliary verb (was not, were not)
before auxiliary verb (not was, not were)
videoQuiz2.explanation: NOT always comes after the auxiliary verb: was not / were not (or contractions: wasn’t / weren’t).

videoQuiz2.text: Are the forms of main verbs the same in positive and negative sentences?
videoQuiz2.explanation:
In Continuous tenses we always add -ing ending to the verbs in all types of sentences.
They were arguing.
They weren’t arguing.
Were they arguing?

videoQuiz2.text: The auxiliary verbs in Past Continuous…
options: change according to the subjects (I, You, He, etc)
is same for all subjects (I, You, He, etc)
videoQuiz2.explanation:
The auxiliary verbs in Past Continuous change according to the subjects.
Iwasn’t playing
Youweren’t playing
Hewasn’t playing
Shewasn’t playing
Itwasn’t playing
Weweren’t playing
Theyweren’t playing

blocks.intro:
The word order in negative Past Continuous sentences is SAV.
Subject + wasn’t/weren’t + verb(ing)

Contractions: wasn’t = was not    weren’t = were not

videoQuiz2b.text: The two girls in glasses ______ at Randy.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: "The two girls" = they (plural) → weren’t looking. Use weren’t with plural subjects; wasn’t is for I/he/she/it; the verb must have -ing.

videoQuiz2b.text: The girl in the blue shirt ______ to be mean.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: "The girl" = she (3rd person singular) → wasn’t trying. Use wasn’t with he/she/it; the verb needs -ing.

videoQuiz2b.text: The football team ______ the field yet, so the girls had to wait.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: "The football team" = it (singular collective) → wasn’t leaving. Use wasn’t for a singular noun; the verb needs -ing.

videoQuiz2b.text: The girl in the purple top ______ a blue shirt like the others.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: "The girl" = she (3rd person singular) → wasn’t wearing. Wasn’t wear is missing -ing; weren’t is incorrect for a singular subject.

videoQuiz2b.text: The girls ______ at each other; they were looking at the popular girl.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: "The girls" = they (plural) → weren’t looking. Wasn’t is for singular; the verb always needs -ing.

videoQuiz3.text:
Focus on the question that the interviewer asks Chris: “What were you doing before you were arrested?”
Match the verbs with their definitions:
definitions: i. the action in progress that was interrupted by another short action
ii. the short action that interrupted another action in progress
videoQuiz3.explanation:
We also use the Past Continuous to show that one action in progress was interrupted by another short action.
Chris was painting his house when he got arrested.

videoQuiz3.text:
What word starts the question?
“What were you doing before you were arrested?”
videoQuiz3.explanation:
What is a WH-question word. WH-question words stay at the beginning of interrogative sentences.
What were you doing yesterday evening?
What was she doing when you saw her?

videoQuiz3.text: The auxiliary verbs in interrogative Past Continuous sentences stay…
options: before the subject  (Was I, Were you, etc.)
after the subject  (I was, You were, etc.)
videoQuiz3.explanation:
In questions the auxiliary verb comes before the subject.
Word order: Was/Were + subject + verb+ing?

videoQuiz3.text: Put the words in the correct order:
Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz3.correct: What were you drawing at your art lesson?
videoQuiz3.explanation:
The word order in interrogative Past Continuous sentences is ASV (Was/Were + subject + verb+ing).
Were you dancing?   Was he crying?

blocks.intro:
We also use the Past Continuous to show that one action in progress was interrupted by another short action.
I was sleeping when the door knocked.
My mom was still cooking dinner when I came home.
examples: The word order for interrogative Past Continuous sentences is ASV: Was/Were + subject + verb+ing?
WH-questions stay at the beginning of interrogative sentences.
Was I snoring when I was sleeping?
What were you talking to the teacher?

videoQuiz3b.text: ______ Chris ______ his apartment before he was arrested?
videoQuiz3b.explanation: "Chris" = he (3rd person singular) → Was; the verb needs -ing → painting.

videoQuiz3b.text: ______ the men in suits ______ to Chris’s story?
______ Chris ______ his hands while he explained the situation?
______ everyone ______ at the table during the interview?
______ Chris ______ from the police station to the office?
______ Chris ______ a suit during the interview?
______ Chris ______ about his chances of getting the job?

exercises.question: Выберите правильное предложение:
options: I was working when he arrived.
I were working when he arrived.
I am working when he arrived.

exercises.question: Дополните: "She __ sleeping when the phone rang."
exercises.explanation: С "she" в Past Continuous: was sleeping.

exercises.question: Выберите правильное предложение:
options: They were playing chess all evening.
They was playing chess all evening.
They are playing chess all evening.
They have been playing chess all evening.
exercises.explanation: С "they" в Past Continuous: were playing.

exercises.question: Дополните отрицание: "They __ not __ cooking when I came."
exercises.explanation: С "they" в отрицании: were not cooking.

exercises.question: Какой вопрос грамматически верен?
exercises.explanation: Past Continuous вопрос: Were + you + doing?

exercises.question: Завершите вопрос: "__ she __ reading when you called?"
exercises.explanation: С "she" в Past Continuous вопросе: Was she reading?

exercises.question: Выберите правильное использование Past Continuous:
options: When I arrived, they were eating dinner.
When I arrived, they eat dinner.
When I arrive, they were eating dinner.
When I arrive, they eating dinner.
exercises.explanation: Past Continuous для фонового действия: were eating.

exercises.question: Найдите ошибку:
options: They was waiting for the bus.
I was getting ready for work.

exercises.question: Выберите правильный вариант:
options: What were they talking about at 5 PM?
What they were talking about at 5 PM?
They were talking what at 5 PM?
What was they talking about at 5 PM?
exercises.explanation: Past Continuous вопрос: What were they talking about?

exercises.question: Какой вариант содержит ошибку?
options: While I was working, she called me.
It was raining when we left.
They were not sleeping all night.
He were building a house for months.

exercises.question: Какое предложение правильно использует Past Continuous?
options: I was watch a movie when the doorbell rang.
I was watching a movie when the doorbell rang.
I watched a movie when the doorbell rang.
I were watching a movie when the doorbell rang.

exercises.question: Какой вопрос требует ответа "Yes, I was"?
options: Were you working when I called?

exercises.question: Дополните: "While they __ __ football, it __ to rain."
Выберите правильный порядок в Past Continuous вопросе:
options: How long were you waiting for me?
How long you were waiting for me?
How long were waiting for me you?
Wait how long were you for me?
exercises.explanation: Past Continuous вопрос: Question word + Were + you + V+ing?
